content.full_text:
PHILIP MERING
ATTORNEY AT LAW
COURT PLAZA BUILDING
901 H STREET, SUITE 604
SACRAMENTO, CALIFORNIA 95814
TELEPHONE 442-0794

February 5, 1991


Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street, Suite 88
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804-0807

Dear Sir:

     I am the City Attorney for the City of Isleton.  The

population of the City is approximately 900 and it is

approximately 285 acres in size.  I am writing to request an

advice letter concerning the following two matters:

     1.  In the City there is a four (4) block section

which is known as Chinatown or Asian American Historic District

("Historic District").  In this area property owners are

eligible for facade grants to assist in the improvement of store

fronts.  The grant program is administered by the Sacramento

Housing and Redevelopment Agency.  Facade designs are submitted

to the Isleton Planning Commission for compliance with written

Design Review Standards adopted by the Isleton Redevelopment

agency.  Two of the members of the Planning Commission own

property within the Historic District.  One of the property

owners operates a computer business from his property.  Are

either or both of them precluded from participating in the

facade review program?

F.P.P.C.
February 5, 1991
Page Two


     2.  The City has adopted a nuisance abatement

ordinance which prohibits the maintenance of buildings or

structures in a deteriorated condition.  The ordinance

establishes a procedure which calls for a hearing before the

Planning Commission, with a right of appeal to the City Council.

A copy of the ordinance is enclosed.  Although the abatement

ordinance is not confined to the Historic District, the great

majority of buildings which the ordinance regulates are located

in the Historic District.  Are Planning Commissioners who own

property or do business within the Historic District precluded

from participating in the hearing on the abatement of structures

within the District?  Is a member of the City

Council/Redevelopement Agency owning property within the

Historic District, precluded from acting on an appeal of a

Planning Commission decision regarding the abatement of a

structure located within the District?

          If you have questions or require additional

information, please let me hear from you.

                                        Very truly yours,

                                        [signature]
                                        Philip Mering

PM:pmm
